title: Genotyping and Phylogenetic Analysis with ViroTrakr Workflows
author: Jayanthi Gangiredla
materials: Reference libraries are available in the history. 3.1.

steps.title: Create a GalaxyTrakr account if you are the first-time user
steps.instruction: Create a GalaxyTrakr account if you are the first-time user: Galaxy | GT Prod 🧬🔬

steps.title: Log into your GalaxyTrakr account if you already have one
steps.instruction: Log into your GalaxyTrakr account if you already have one: Galaxy | GT Prod 🧬🔬

steps.title: Get familiar with GalaxyTrakr components
steps.instruction: Get familiar with GalaxyTrakr components: Tools, Information and History:

steps.title: Upload assembled sequences
steps.instruction: Upload assembled sequences. This ViroTrakr workflow supports both raw sequencing data and pre-assembled sequences. You canupload raw data directly (Item 3), or use assembled sequences generated on other platforms and stored in your local folder.

steps.title: Upload raw sequencing data and execute the data analysis workflow
steps.instruction: Upload raw sequencing data and execute the data analysis workflow. The raw sequencing data in fastq files can be imported into GalaxyTrakr directly from your local folder (instructions shown in 3.1); or downloaded from SRA (instructions shown in 3.2) if the files have been already submitted to ViroTrakr in NCBI (following the Submission protocol): NCBI submission protocol for foodborne virus surveillance (protocols.io) ).After being uploaded to GalaxyTrakr, the files will remain in your account and stay private until they are deleted.

steps.title: Click on the button “Upload”
steps.instruction: Click on the button “Upload”. 3.1.1.-cont. Click “Choose local files”. 3.1.

steps.title: Select fastq files from your local folder, then click “Open”
steps.instruction: Select fastq files from your local folder, then click “Open”. 3.1.

steps.title: Click “Start” to upload
steps.instruction: Click “Start” to upload. 3.1.

steps.title: Check the status of data upload
steps.instruction: Check the status of data upload. 3.1.

steps.title: List of Datasets will be available in the history
steps.instruction: List of Datasets will be available in the history. 3.1.

steps.title: Import the reference data files from...
steps.instruction: Import the reference data files from the Shared Data libraries following the steps below. 3.1.8.-cont. Import the reference data files from the Shared Data libraries following the steps below. 3.1.

steps.title: Click the “WorkFlows” button in the...
steps.instruction: Click the “WorkFlows” button in the left-hand menu -> select the "Public" workflows -> then type and search for “ViroTrakr”. 3.1.10.-cont. Select the appropriate workflow (“Norovirus with reads as input” in this case), then click the arrowhead button to Run the workflow. 3.1.

steps.title: Ensure to select all the appropriate...
steps.instruction: Ensure to select all the appropriate files (arrows) from each dropdown menu and Run Workflow. 3.1.

steps.title: The workflow status will be displayed step-by-step in real time
steps.instruction: The workflow status will be displayed step-by-step in real time. As each step completes, it will turn green in both the Steps and History panels. The run may take a few minutes or longer, depending on the data and system load. 3.1.12.-cont. Once the run is complete, the results will be available in the individual reports in the History. Users can view and download each output file, including the assembly, viral contigs, genotyping report and phylogenetic tree, etc. 3.1.

steps.title: An example of a genotyping report is shown below
steps.instruction: An example of a genotyping report is shown below: 3.1.

steps.title: An example of a phylogenetic analysis report is shown below
steps.instruction: An example of a phylogenetic analysis report is shown below: The result files include: •Assembly with MEGAHIT: Metagenomic assembly generated using MEGAHIT. •Report: Kraken

steps.title: Kraken2 classification reports. •Report_blasthits_Genotype
steps.instruction: Kraken2 classification reports. •Report_blasthits_Genotype: Report of the best BLAST Hits against reference sequences. •Genotyping_report: Final report contains QC statistics and genotyping results. •Virus contigs: Target virus specific contigs extracted from the metagenomics assembly. •Reference_query_phylogenetic_tree: Phylogenetic tree showing the input genomes alongside reference genomes from all groups (available in.png and .txt formats).

steps.title: Create a list of SRA accession numbers. * Note
steps.instruction: Create a list of SRA accession numbers. * Note: the numbers must start with SRR, DRR or ERR in a text file, one per line. Save it in a local folder. 3.2.2. (
